# Transcrição: 15-Homelessness and the Goat

- **Fonte:** 15-Homelessness and the Goat
- **Duração:** 07:42
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

I'm walking on a 7-Eleven with my friend the other day and we see a guy in the parking lot And he is brushing his teeth up against his car And when we get in my car my friend wonders aloud like why the hell is there a guy in 7-Eleven brushing his teeth in the parking lot at 9 o'clock at night I don't say anything, but I know exactly what he is doing because I was in exactly the same position at a point in my life It's 1993 I'm 23 years old and I'm living in Somerville, but living in Somerville might be stretching it a bit I would tell you that I'm homeless But sunny the Vietnam vet who sleeps on the corner near the movie theater tells me that since I'm in my car I have a roof and if you have a roof you ain't homeless Six months before I'm living in Atalboro in a two-bedroom townhouse with an indoor pool and tennis courts and a health center I'm living with my best friend Benji. He's just graduated from college and he's looking for a job. I'm working for McDonald's I'm managing McDonald's restaurants and making my way up the corporate ladder when two things happen that changed my life First Benji gets that job. He was looking for but it's not in Massachusetts. It's in Connecticut. So he's moving and Then a $7,000 deposit at the McDonald's where I'm working disappears It's one that I'm responsible for and though my bosses don't think I took it and I didn't we run into a police officer Who thinks I did and very quickly I am arrested. I'm indicted. I lose my job I can't collect unemployment because of the reason I've been fired and I can't get any job myself. I End up homeless because the people who can help me don't and the people who can't help me won't My mother has muscular dystrophy and she's living in a assisted living facility I haven't seen my father for 15 years My sister is a teenage mother whose couch surfing and barely surviving and my brother has gone to the military And we haven't heard from him in years My friend Sherry offers me a loan of a thousand dollars to get back on my feet and I say no I'm gonna be fine. I don't need your money But when things start to go sideways and I think I'm gonna need it I asked her about the loan and she says oh and she says words after the oh, but the oh is the word that I know Means the loan wasn't for real Benji offers to take me to Connecticut with him But I can't leave the state because I'm awaiting my trial and then my friend Allen offers me his other bedroom and his apartment but two days before I'm gonna move in his fiance gets back together with him and He tells me I can't move in I Tell him no big deal. I have a place to stay. I'll be fine I tell all of my friends that I'm fine Don't worry and then I quietly move into my car and head to Somerville I choose Somerville because it has excellent parking at night And it's well lit it makes it impossible to sleep in the light But when you're homeless it turns out that being safe is more important than being asleep

## [03:03 - 06:07]

There's also work in 93 in Somerville that I can get I can work at construction sites I can pick up the scraps and I can run nails and screws to guys who know how to build things and There's a perfume company in the center of town that lets me take their samples down to the streets And I can try to sell them and if I sell them I get a dollar a bottle I can't get a job because you can't get a job without an address But even worse you can't get a job if you don't have a phone and in 1993 you need to own a wall to have a phone that sits on that wall So that your employer can call you and I can't get a wall because I'm living in my car After work every day. I drive to Brockton to Massa Soya Community College to get a shower I have an ex-girlfriend who used to go there so I kind of know the college and I feel at home there There are showers like much closer to Somerville But the 30 miles that I drive to Brockton is the best part of my day Because the thing I didn't know about not having a house is how boring your life becomes so quickly Like you lose TV and you're not going to the movies But without a phone you can't call your friends and without an address they can't visit you and Suddenly you are alone and no one can reach you and you can't reach them One night after my shower I stopped for the McDonald's in Brockton It's one that I used to manage and Mary is the person taking my order Now Mary is a 50 year old Jehovah witness. She is the sweetest woman you've ever met Her husband Jerry is actually the custodian at the McDonald's. I'm the one who hired him. He still works there I'm sitting in the in the restaurant eating my quarter pounder with cheese When Mary comes over and she sits across from me and she says so where are you living now? And I say I'm I'm living with people and I got friends and I'm fine And then Mary like she stares right through me and she says where are you? I'm literally living Mary somehow knows now I find out later that that ex-girlfriend figured out I was living in my car and she told Mary And as I'm looking at Mary I realize I haven't told anyone in the whole world that I'm homeless There's not a person When I was a little boy, we had no money and I spent my entire childhood Either pretending that I had things that I didn't or pretending that I didn't want things that I did You know, I did everything I could to hide the poverty of my childhood And now I'm 23 years old and I'm in the exact same position. I was as a little boy I can't take care of myself and I don't even have a roof and I realize I haven't even said this to myself Every morning I wake up in the backseat of my car and I say to myself. This is the last day. I'm not homeless I'm just waiting for a place But I know in my heart in my mind that I'm not going to get a place That I can't imagine a way out of this And so I decide to tell Mary that I'm living in my car and she tells me you're coming home with me

## [06:07 - 07:42]

And I say no, it's fine. I'm you know, I got a plan, but in my head. I am thinking thank God Thank God that she's going to save me And so we go to her house It's the easiest move in the world because everything is already packed in the car And we get to her house and she's got a pantry off the kitchen and there are two Army cots in it and one of them already has a guy in it. His name is Rick. He is a Jehovah Witness He's like me has no place to go. He wants to be a minister someday He speaks in tongues in his sleep every single night. I Share this room with Rick and Mary and Jerry's indoor pet goat The goat actually had the room before me and Rick so we're actually taking it away from the goat That goat at night if I move my feet will bite my toes And if I sleep too long in the morning it gnaws on my hair until it's a matted mess and If I'm in bed when the sun comes up that goat puts its tongue in my ear to get me to wake up I Lie down on that bed on that first night that army caught and it is lumpy and It is hard and it is uncomfortable And it is the best bed I have ever slept in my entire life. Thank you You
